commit.message:
Only reset USB device on crash recovery, not on every startup (#385)

* Fix false crash detection after intentional sleep shutdown

After shutdown() terminates rtlamr and rtltcp during the sleep cycle,
the process variables still hold references to the dead Popen objects.
On the next loop iteration, poll() detects a returncode and treats
the intentional termination as an unexpected crash, triggering:

1. 'CRITICAL: RTL_TCP has died' false alarm on every cycle
2. Unnecessary USB device reset via the crash-recovery path
3. 'CRITICAL: RTLAMR has died' false alarm
4. An extra sleep_for delay (the crash-recovery path for rtlamr
   sleeps for sleep_for seconds before restarting, doubling the
   actual cycle time)

The fix sets rtlamr and rtltcp to None after intentional shutdown,
so the next iteration creates fresh processes via the normal startup
path instead of the crash-recovery path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Only reset USB device on crash recovery, not on every startup

start_rtltcp() unconditionally calls reset_usb_device() every time
it starts rtl_tcp. With sleep_for=60, this means ~1,440 USB resets
per day during normal operation.

The Linux kernel docs warn that frequent USBDEVFS_RESET calls can
trigger driver and USB subsystem bugs, particularly with lower-quality
devices like many RTL-SDR dongles, potentially destabilizing the USB
connection over time.

Add a reset_usb parameter (default False) to start_rtltcp(). Only
pass True from the crash-recovery path where a USB reset is actually
needed to recover a stuck device.

Fixes #384

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: rewrite trimDots to avoid repeated string allocations (#8154)

* perf: rewrite trimDots to use index scanning instead of repeated string allocation

The old implementation created a new string on each iteration via substr/slice.
The new implementation scans for the first and last non-dot indices using
charCodeAt, then performs a single substring call. If no trimming is needed,
the original string is returned directly (zero allocations).

Benchmark (20M calls across 10 representative inputs):
- Old: ~628 ms
- New: ~154 ms
- ~4x faster (75% reduction)

commit.message:
Fix mounted pages ratcheting during streaming

During multi-turn agentic streaming, the typing indicator turn is
repeatedly added (turn start) and removed (turn end) from the
transcript. Each add triggers RebuildPages which may push page
count above MaxMountedPages, causing TrimMountedHeadOverflow to
increment _firstMountedPageIndex. But when the typing turn is
removed and pages decrease, _firstMountedPageIndex was never
decremented — it permanently ratcheted up over multiple sub-turns,
progressively unmounting earlier pages containing user messages
and assistant responses.

Fix: after TrimMountedHeadOverflow, backfill earlier pages whenever
MountedPageCount is below MaxMountedPages. This ensures freed slots
from removed turns are reclaimed instead of lost.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Avoid early-stopping/checkpoint monitor failures on skipped validation epochs (#848)

* Avoid train-epoch monitor checks without validation metrics
* fix: guard on_validation_end against absent metrics on non-eval epochs

With `save_on_train_epoch_end=False` (set by the previous commit),
PTL 2.6.1 routes all checkpoint saving through `on_validation_end`.
On non-eval epochs `COCOEvalCallback` returns early without logging
`val/mAP_50_95`, leaving `callback_metrics` empty for that key.
The unguarded `super().on_validation_end()` call then raised
`MisconfigurationException` — effectively moving the crash from
`on_train_epoch_end` to `on_validation_end` rather than eliminating it.

Fix: return early in `BestModelCallback.on_validation_end` when the
monitor key is absent from `callback_metrics`.

Also add:
- Unit test `test_validation_end_ignores_missing_validation_metrics`
  covering the now-guarded path directly.
- Smoke test `test_eval_interval_does_not_crash` running a real
  `Trainer.fit()` for 3 epochs with metrics logged only every 2nd
  epoch, exercising both crash paths end-to-end.
- Docstring notes on `BestModelCallback` and `RFDETREarlyStopping`
  that checkpointing/stopping evaluates only on validation epochs.


---------

commit.message:
perf: optimize now() with integer counter tracking (#8152)

* perf: optimize now() by replacing Math.round with integer counter tracking

Replace floating point arithmetic and Math.round(ret * 100) / 100 rounding
with separate integer tracking of milliseconds and sub-millisecond counter.
This eliminates the need for Math.round() entirely and reduces floating point
operations, achieving ~4.5% faster execution in benchmarks.

Before (1M calls): 56.04ms median

commit.message:
fix: ensure browser test error logs always include a stack trace (#8156)

When certain error types like DOMException are thrown in browser tests,
error.stack can be undefined even though instanceof Error passes.
This changes the handlers to:

commit.message:
perf: optimize ensureNotFalsy by separating throw into helper function (#8155)

Move the throw statement into a separate `ensureNotFalsyThrow` helper
function so that V8's TurboFan JIT can more aggressively inline the
main `ensureNotFalsy` function at call sites. The hot path (truthy
values) now has a simpler control flow graph without throw, which
improves inlining heuristics.

Benchmark results (200M iterations, 9 runs, median):
- Before: 628.2 ms
- After:  603.4 ms
- Improvement: ~4% faster

commit.message:
Fix first user message invisible in short chats

TranscriptTurnControl.OnTurnChanged fires when the ItemsControl
sets the container's DataContext via binding, which happens BEFORE
OnAttachedToVisualTree. RebuildItemHosts creates ContentPresenter
children whose DataTemplate resolution walks UP the visual tree to
find templates defined in ChatView.axaml. When the control isn't
attached yet, the lookup fails silently — the presenters get no
template content and measure at zero height, leaving the turn
invisible.

OnAttachedToVisualTree calls RebuildItemHosts again, but by then
Avalonia's layout engine may not re-measure if it considers the
children structurally unchanged (same ContentPresenter type, same
Content reference).

Fix: skip RebuildItemHosts in OnTurnChanged when the control isn't
attached to the visual tree. OnAttachedToVisualTree already calls
RebuildItemHosts unconditionally, so the children are built exactly
once — after the tree connection is established and template
resolution can succeed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
